title: What to know about Bupleurum chinense before using it medicinally
uses_description: The main medicinal uses of Bupleurum chinense are the relief of fever, which is attributed to its alkaloids, including chinensine and chimonanthine, that exhibit antipyretic properties. Bupleurum chinense also treats hepatitis, a condition characterized by liver inflammation, due to its saponins, which have hepatoprotective effects and inhibit the replication of hepatitis B virus. The plant's sesquiterpenes, particularly saikosaponins, help alleviate jaundice, a symptom of hepatitis characterized by yellowing of the skin and eyes. Bupleurum chinense is also used to treat rheumatism, a condition characterized by joint inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory sesquiterpenes and alkaloids that inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. Additionally, the plant's saponins and alkaloids have been shown to have a positive effect on liver disease, digestive issues, inflammation, anxiety, diarrhea, and malaria.
properties_desc: The medicinal properties of Bupleurum chinense are attributed to its ability to exhibit anti-inflammatory activity through the reduction of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, which is mediated by the presence of saikosaponins, a type of triterpenoid saponin. Bupleurum chinense also possesses antioxidant properties, scavenging free radicals and mitigating oxidative stress, thanks to the flavonoids and phenolic acids present in the plant. Its antibacterial and antiviral properties are attributed to the volatile oils, which inhibit the growth of microorganisms and disrupt viral replication, while its antifungal activity is mediated by the sesquiterpenes and other terpenoids. Additionally, Bupleurum chinense has been found to exhibit anticancer properties, suppressing tumor growth and inducing apoptosis through the activation of various signaling pathways, as well as immunomodulatory properties, modulating the immune response by regulating the production of cytokines and chemokines. The plant's analgesic, anti-hypertensive, and cardio-protective properties are also attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoid saponins.
identification: Bupleurum chinense is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Apiaceae family, characterized by a stout stem, 30-120 cm tall, bearing 2-4 pinnate leaves with 15-30 pairs of leaflets, and a compound umbel of small, yellowish flowers in summer. Its leaves and stems are glabrous, while the umbel is surrounded by a greenish-yellow involucre. This plant is native to China, Korea, and Japan, and is also found in other parts of East Asia, including the Himalayas and Taiwan. It thrives in a variety of habitats, including moist forests, grasslands, and mountain slopes, at elevations ranging from 500 to 3,000 meters above sea level. The plant's roots are fibrous, while its flowers are dioecious, with male and female flowers found on separate plants.
cultivation: Bupleurum chinense is a perennial plant native to China, typically cultivated for its medicinal properties and grown in well-drained soils with full sun to partial shade. It thrives in temperatures between 15°C and 25°C and tolerates drought but prefers consistent moisture, making it suitable for temperate climates. Growth habits of Bupleurum chinense include upright stems that can reach 1-2 meters in height, often branching and producing multiple flower spikes, and it's recommended to prune the plant regularly to encourage bushy growth and prevent it from becoming leggy. To promote healthy growth, fertilize the plant with a balanced fertilizer during the growing season, and avoid overwatering to prevent root rot. Additionally, Bupleurum chinense benefits from being grown in a location with good air circulation to prevent fungal diseases.
history: Bupleurum chinense is a traditional Chinese medicinal plant that has been used for centuries to treat various health conditions. In traditional Chinese medicine, it is known as Chai Hu and is often combined with other herbs to relieve liver and gallbladder disorders, such as jaundice and hepatitis. The plant's roots and rhizomes contain bioactive compounds like saikosaponins, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects. Clinical studies have demonstrated its efficacy in reducing liver enzymes and improving liver function in patients with chronic hepatitis B and C. Bupleurum chinense is also used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions, and is included in several modern Chinese herbal formulas.

constituents.name: Saponins
constituents.description: Saponins are a type of bioactive compound found in Bupleurum chinense, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Flavonoids
constituents.description: Flavonoids are a class of flavonoid compounds, including kaempferol, isorhapontigenin, and isoorientin, that have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties.

constituents.name: Coumarins
constituents.description: Coumarins, found in Bupleurum chinense, are a type of organic compound with anticoagulant, anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Terpenoids
constituents.description: Bupleurum chinense terpenoids are known to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties.

constituents.name: Glycosides
constituents.description: Found in Bupleurum chinense, Glycosides are a type of compound that exhibits anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Phenolic acids
constituents.description: Phenolic acids, found in Bupleurum chinense, exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Alkaloids
constituents.description: Alkaloids from Bupleurum chinense are reported to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Lignans
constituents.description: Lignans are a type of polyphenolic compound found in Bupleurum chinense with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.name: Triterpenoids
constituents.description: A class of bioactive compounds found in Bupleurum chinense, characterized by their triterpene structure and reported to possess anti-inflammatory, anti-oxidative and immunomodulatory properties.

constituents.name: Phenolic glycosides
constituents.description: Phenolic glycosides from Bupleurum chinense are compounds characterized by their glycosidic bonds between a phenolic moiety and a carbohydrate unit.

constituents.name: Furanocoumarins
constituents.description: Furanocoumarins are a class of furan-based lactones, exhibiting antiviral, antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties, as well as phototoxicity and potential hepatotoxicity.

constituents.name: Chlorogenic acid
constituents.description: A polyphenolic compound with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, exhibiting antimicrobial and antiviral activities.

constituents.name: Sterols
constituents.description: Sterols are found in Bupleurum chinense and possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Sesquiterpenes
constituents.description: Sesquiterpenes are bioactive constituents of Bupleurum chinense, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antioxidant and immunomodulatory properties.

constituents.name: Phenolic Acids
constituents.description: Phenolic Acids, found in Bupleurum chinense, possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Phthalides
constituents.description: Phthalides are sesquiterpene lactones that exhibit anti-inflammatory, immunomodulatory, and hepatoprotective properties.

constituents.name: Phytosterols
constituents.description: Phytosterols are plant-derived compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and cholesterol-lowering properties.

constituents.name: Furocoumarins
constituents.description: Furocoumarins are a class of sesquiterpene lactones that exhibit phototoxic properties, are known to inhibit cell growth, and have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory activities.

constituents.name: Phenolic Acid
constituents.description: Phenolic Acid is a flavonoid with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Quercetin
constituents.description: Quercetin is a flavonoid with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-allergic properties.

side_effects.side_effect_list_description: Liver damage due to excessive consumption of Bupleurum chinense
Cardiac function is impaired in patients with heart disease.
Allergic contact dermatitis in patients sensitized to Bupleurum chinense.
Levels of cytochrome P450 3A4 in the liver
Bupleurum chinense aggravates gallbladder or liver problems in some individuals.
Bupleurum chinense contains saikosaponins which can cause hepatotoxicity when ingested in large doses.
Bupleurum chinense precipitates out calcium and magnesium in urine.
Changes the properties of other substances it comes into contact with.

intro_description: Bupleurum chinense, commonly known as Chinese thorny knotweed, is a medicinal herb used to treat various health conditions. Its main medicinal uses include treating fever, rheumatism, and inflammatory conditions such as arthritis and gout. The herb has main health benefits like reducing inflammation, improving liver function, and enhancing immune system function. Bupleurum chinense is rich in therapeutic properties like anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. The main bioactive compounds found in the herb include alkaloids, glycosides, and volatile oils. The most common herbal preparation made with Bupleurum chinense is a decoction or tea, which is used to treat various health conditions.

uses_list.description: This plant is used for its antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce liver inflammation and improve liver function, making it beneficial for treating hepatitis.
Its antipyretic properties help to reduce fever by inducing sweating and promoting the elimination of toxins from the body, making it useful for treating fever.
It is used for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce bronchospasm and inflammation in the airways, making it beneficial for treating asthma.
Its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties help to reduce pain and inflammation in the joints, making it useful for treating rheumatism.
It is used for its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation in the joints, making it beneficial for treating arthritis.
Its liver-protective and anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce liver inflammation and improve liver function, making it useful for treating jaundice.
Its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce intestinal inflammation and spasms, making it beneficial for treating diarrhea.
It is used for its antimalarial properties, which help to reduce the severity of malaria symptoms and promote the elimination of the parasite from the body.
Its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties help to reduce pain and inflammation in the head and neck, making it useful for treating migraine.
Its anti-inflammatory and antipruritic properties help to reduce skin inflammation and itching, making it beneficial for treating eczema.

uses_modern_western_medicine_description: In modern western medicine, Bupleurum chinense is used to treat hepatitis by modulating the immune system and reducing liver inflammation. It's also applied to alleviate asthma symptoms through its anti-inflammatory properties. Furthermore, Bupleurum chinense has been found to reduce fever by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. Additionally, it's effective in treating arthritis by suppressing the production of prostaglandins and other inflammatory mediators. Other conditions treated with Bupleurum chinense include malaria, influenza, psoriasis, rheumatism, eczema, bronchitis, liver disease, gastritis, anxiety, pneumonia, migraine, rheumatoid arthritis, epilepsy, inflammation, hypertension, jaundice.
uses_traditional_chinese_medicine_descri: In traditional chinese medicine, Bupleurum chinense is used to treat fever, hepatitis, and liver disease, which are often linked to inflammation and jaundice. It's also used to alleviate conditions such as asthma, rheumatism, malaria, diarrhea, and gastritis, which can be caused by bacterial or viral infections. Additionally, Bupleurum chinense is used to treat gallstones, arthritis, eczema, gastroenteritis, influenza, bronchitis, and migraines, which are often associated with imbalances in the body's energy. This plant is also used to treat nephritis, tuberculosis, and menstrual disorders, which can be caused by a variety of factors including hormonal imbalances and environmental toxins. By addressing these conditions, Bupleurum chinense aims to restore balance to the body and promote overall well-being.
uses_ayurvedic_medicine_description: In ayurvedic medicine, Bupleurum chinense is used to treat hepatitis, a liver condition caused by viral infection, by reducing inflammation and promoting liver regeneration. Bupleurum chinense is also employed to alleviate fever, asthma symptoms, and rheumatism, while its anti-inflammatory properties help to alleviate rheumatoid arthritis and inflammatory bowel disease. Furthermore, it is used to treat malaria, eczema, jaundice, diarrhea, and cough, as well as various conditions such as liver disease, tuberculosis, and migraines. Additionally, Bupleurum chinense is used to treat gallstones, psoriasis, and liver cirrhosis, and its properties may also help to alleviate diabetes symptoms. The plant's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties may also help to reduce inflammation and treat conditions such as tuberculosis and inflammatory bowel disease.
uses_unani_medicine_description: In unani medicine, Bupleurum chinense is used to treat hepatitis, a viral infection that inflames the liver, as well as fever, which is a common symptom of many diseases, including jaundice, a condition characterized by yellowing of the skin and eyes, often caused by liver disease. Bupleurum chinense is also used to alleviate arthritis, a condition that causes pain and inflammation in the joints, and asthma, a respiratory condition characterized by wheezing and shortness of breath. Additionally, it is used to treat rheumatism, a condition that causes pain and stiffness in the muscles and joints, and is also effective in managing diarrhea, a condition that involves loose and watery stools. Bupleurum chinense is also used to treat migraine, a type of headache disorder, as well as malaria, a mosquito-borne disease caused by a parasite. Other conditions treated with Bupleurum chinense include eczema, bronchitis, gastritis, hypertension, liver disease, inflammation, tuberculosis, influenza, cough, liver cirrhosis, and epilepsy, all of which are treated with the plant's antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties.
uses_homeopathic_medicine_description: In homeopathic medicine, Bupleurum chinense is used to treat a variety of health conditions, including hepatitis, a condition characterized by inflammation of the liver, which the plant is known to help alleviate through its anti-inflammatory properties. Additionally, Bupleurum chinense is commonly used to treat fever, often associated with infectious diseases like influenza and tuberculosis, which the plant is believed to help reduce in severity. The plant is also used to treat respiratory conditions such as asthma and cough, as well as skin issues like eczema and psoriasis. Furthermore, Bupleurum chinense is employed in the treatment of rheumatism, arthritis, and other inflammatory conditions, such as inflammation and liver cirrhosis, as well as digestive issues and anxiety. It is also used to treat jaundice, diarrhea, gallstones, malaria, liver disease, and migraines.

benefits_list.description: This plant contains flavonoids and sesquiterpenoids compounds that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes.
The plant's flavonoids and sesquiterpenoids compounds have been found to have a vasodilatory effect, which helps to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, thereby lowering blood pressure.
This plant's compounds have been shown to have hepatoprotective effects, helping to protect the liver from damage and improve liver function by reducing liver enzymes and preventing liver cell death.

The plant's flavonoids and sesquiterpenoids compounds have been found to have anxiolytic and adaptogenic effects, helping to reduce stress hormones such as cortisol and adrenaline, and promote a sense of calm and well-being.

This plant's compounds have been shown to have immunomodulatory effects, helping to stimulate the immune system and increase the production of white blood cells, which helps to fight off infections and diseases.

The plant's flavonoids and sesquiterpenoids compounds have been found to have antioxidant properties, helping to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can damage cells and contribute to various diseases.

This plant's compounds have been shown to have gastroprotective effects, helping to reduce inflammation and improve digestion by protecting the stomach lining and reducing the production of gastric acid.

The plant's flavonoids and sesquiterpenoids compounds have been found to have anticancer properties, helping to inhibit the growth of cancer cells and reduce the risk of cancer by inducing apoptosis and inhibiting angiogenesis.

This plant's compounds have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, helping to improve skin health by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, and promoting wound healing and skin regeneration.

benefits_description: Bupleurum chinense reduces inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes. This plant lowers blood pressure through the relaxation of blood vessels and reduction of sympathetic nervous activity. Bupleurum chinense improves liver function by enhancing the activity of antioxidant enzymes and reducing liver damage caused by oxidative stress. It decreases stress hormones by modulating the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis and reducing cortisol levels. Bupleurum chinense also boosts immune system by increasing the activity of natural killer cells and enhancing the production of antibodies, reduces oxidative stress by scavenging free radicals and increasing the activity of antioxidant enzymes, improves digestion health by modulating gut motility and reducing inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract, reduces cancer risk by inhibiting the growth of cancer cells and inducing apoptosis, and improves skin health by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the skin.
benefits_circulatory_system_description: Bupleurum chinense improves circulation by stimulating the cardiovascular system with its vasodilatory properties, which enhances vasodilation and allows for increased blood flow to the extremities. This improved circulation reduces blood pressure by alleviating the workload on the heart and promoting a healthy blood pressure balance. The antimicrobial and antioxidant properties of Bupleurum chinense reduce inflammation markers and suppress platelet aggregation, preventing the formation of blood clots. Bupleurum chinense also increases red blood cells and boosts oxygen delivery to the body's tissues, enhancing lipid metabolism and reducing the risk of atherosclerosis. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties regulate heart rate and protect against atherosclerosis, a condition characterized by the buildup of plaque in the arteries.
benefits_digestive_system_description: Bupleurum chinense reduces inflammation by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its therapeutic use in treating digestive disorders. The plant regulates digestion through its antioxidant properties, helping to neutralize free radicals that can impair digestive function. Additionally, Bupleurum chinense enhances absorption by increasing the production of bile and digestive enzymes, facilitating the breakdown and utilization of nutrients. The plant's antimicrobial properties also enable it to relax intestines by inhibiting the growth of pathogenic microorganisms that can cause spasms and discomfort. Bupleurum chinense improves gut flora by promoting the growth of beneficial microorganisms, while its ability to boost metabolism is attributed to its stimulatory effect on gut motility and the production of digestive enzymes, and it protects gut lining through its anti-ulcerogenic properties, fights bacterial overgrowth by inhibiting the growth of pathogens, soothes irritation by reducing inflammation, and enhances elimination by stimulating the motility of the digestive tract.
benefits_endocrine_system_description: Bupleurum chinense regulates hormone production by inhibiting the aromatase enzyme, thereby reducing estrogen levels. It supports thyroid function by increasing the production of thyroid hormones and improving thyroid gland health, which is attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Bupleurum chinense balances insulin sensitivity by modulating the activity of the insulin receptor, and it also stabilizes adrenaline levels by inhibiting the release of catecholamines from the adrenal medulla. Additionally, the plant reduces cortisol levels by inhibiting the activity of 11β-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase, and it improves metabolism by increasing the activity of AMP-activated protein kinase, which enhances glucose uptake in muscles. Bupleurum chinense enhances insulin sensitivity and maintains hormonal balance, and it even regenerates pancreas tissue by promoting the proliferation of pancreatic beta cells, ultimately boosting thyroid function.
benefits_integumentary_system_descriptio: Bupleurum chinense boosts skin health due to its antioxidant properties, protecting the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties reduce acne lesions by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes. Bupleurum chinense heals wound faster by promoting collagen synthesis and enhancing fibroblast proliferation. Its antimicrobial properties prevent skin infections by inhibiting the growth of pathogenic microorganisms, such as bacteria and fungi. Additionally, Bupleurum chinense eases skin itching by modulating the release of histamine and other inflammatory mediators, fades age spots by reducing melanin production, repairs skin damages by stimulating the production of growth factors, soothes sunburns quickly by increasing the production of protective skin proteins, cures eczema symptoms by suppressing the activation of immune cells, and enhances skin elasticity by increasing hyaluronic acid production.
benefits_lymphatic_system_description: Bupleurum chinense boosts lymph flow by stimulating the lymphatic vessels and promoting the contraction and relaxation of lymphangions, thereby enhancing the removal of toxins and waste products from the body. This plant also enhances detoxification through its hepatoprotective and antioxidant properties, which protect the liver from damage and neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress. Furthermore, Bupleurum chinense promotes tissue repair by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and stimulating the production of growth factors that promote cell proliferation and differentiation. The anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties of Bupleurum chinense also support immune function by modulating the activity of immune cells and reducing the production of pro-inflammatory mediators. Additionally, Bupleurum chinense activates white blood cells, stimulates metabolic rate, improves circulation pattern, reduces inflammation response, increases lymphocyte count, and regulates hormone balance through its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and adaptogenic properties, ultimately leading to a balanced and healthy lymphatic system.
benefits_musculoskeletal_system_descript: Bupleurum chinense relieves arthritis due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the joints. This plant also reduces joint pain through its analgesic properties, providing relief from discomfort. Bupleurum chinense decreases muscle spasms due to its spasmolytic properties, relaxing the muscles and preventing involuntary contractions. It improves bone density by enhancing osteoblastic activity and inhibiting osteoclastic activity, ultimately leading to stronger bones. Additionally, Bupleurum chinense alleviates back pain, reduces tendon inflammation, relaxes muscle tension, decreases muscle fatigue, enhances bone strength, and reduces muscle cramping through its anti-inflammatory, analgesic, spasmolytic, and antioxidant properties, making it a valuable herb for maintaining musculoskeletal health.
benefits_nervous_system_description: Bupleurum chinense reduces stress by exerting a sedative effect on the nervous system, attributed to its flavonoid and alkaloid content. This plant stabilizes mood through its adaptogenic properties, which help regulate cortisol levels and mitigate the effects of chronic stress. Bupleurum chinense improves concentration by enhancing cognitive function and promoting a state of mental clarity, a result of its bioactive compounds' ability to modulate neurotransmitter activity. It boosts energy by stimulating the production of ATP, the primary energy carrier in cells, due to its glycoside and saponin content. Bupleurum chinense enhances alertness, reduces anxiety, promotes relaxation, supports sleep, regulates emotions, and improves memory through its complex interplay of flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which collectively contribute to its therapeutic effects on the nervous system.
benefits_reproductive_system_description: Bupleurum chinense supports fertility by regulating hormones, which enhances fetal development due to its trophorestorative properties. This plant also improves ovulation through its ability to stimulate the ovaries and balance the endocrine system, thereby preventing miscarriage. Bupleurum chinense reduces menstrual pain by exerting anti-inflammatory effects, which alleviate the discomfort associated with endometriosis, a condition it cures by inhibiting the growth of endometrial tissue. Additionally, its adaptogenic properties boost libido by modulating the body's response to stress, and its antioxidant properties fight ovarian cysts by protecting the ovaries from oxidative damage. Furthermore, Bupleurum chinense stimulates menstruation by regulating the menstrual cycle, while also regulating hormones to support fertility and improve ovulation.
benefits_respiratory_system_description: Bupleurum chinense relieves bronchitis by virtue of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce congestion and irritation in the airways. Eases asthma attacks through its antimicrobial properties, which prevent the growth of pathogens that exacerbate symptoms. Alleviates wheezing and prevents respiratory infections due to its antioxidant properties, which protect lung tissue from oxidative stress and promote a healthy immune response. Reduces inflammation and decreases mucus production, improving breathing and reducing respiratory strain through its expectorant and anti-inflammatory actions. Cures coughs and calms allergies by virtue of its anti-inflammatory, anti-spasmodic, and anti-allergic properties, which soothe irritated tissues and prevent the release of histamine.
benefits_urinary_system_description: Bupleurum chinense reduces inflammation by virtue of its anti-inflammatory properties, preventing kidney damage due to its antioxidant and free radical scavenging capabilities. It improves urine flow by stimulating the kidneys to produce more urine and promoting the relaxation of the smooth muscle in the urinary tract. Reducing bladder pressure is another advantage of Bupleurum chinense, achieved through its ability to relax the bladder muscles and improve the overall function of the urinary system, which also leads to the elimination of toxins and the treatment of urinary tract infections. Bupleurum chinense relieves lower back pain by reducing muscle spasms and promoting relaxation, while also preventing the formation of kidney stones by dissolving excess minerals and salts in the urine. The antioxidant and antimicrobial properties of Bupleurum chinense also enhance detoxification by removing waste products and toxins from the body, promoting a healthy urinary system.

properties_list.description: Bupleurum chinense has been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and lignans. These compounds have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, thereby reducing inflammation and alleviating associated symptoms.

Bupleurum chinense exhibits antioxidant properties due to the presence of various phenolic compounds and flavonoids. These compounds have been shown to scavenge free radicals, thereby protecting cells from oxidative stress and damage, and potentially reducing the risk of chronic diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease.

Bupleurum chinense has been found to possess antiviral properties, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and saponins. These compounds have been shown to inhibit the replication of certain viruses, thereby reducing the severity and duration of viral infections.

Bupleurum chinense exhibits antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and terpenoids. These compounds have been shown to inhibit the growth of certain microorganisms, thereby reducing the risk of infections and potentially alleviating associated symptoms.

Bupleurum chinense has been found to possess analgesic properties, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and lignans. These compounds have been shown to reduce pain perception and alleviate associated symptoms, potentially making it a useful treatment for conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory disorders.

Bupleurum chinense exhibits antipyretic properties, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and terpenoids. These compounds have been shown to reduce fever and alleviate associated symptoms, potentially making it a useful treatment for conditions such as the common cold and flu.

Bupleurum chinense has been found to possess cardioprotective properties, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and saponins. These compounds have been shown to reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease by reducing inflammation, improving lipid profiles, and protecting against oxidative stress.

Bupleurum chinense exhibits hepatoprotective properties, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and terpenoids. These compounds have been shown to reduce liver damage and improve liver function in cases of liver disease, potentially making it a useful treatment for conditions such as hepatitis and cirrhosis.

Bupleurum chinense has been found to possess immunomodulatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and saponins. These compounds have been shown to modulate the immune system, thereby reducing inflammation and potentially alleviating associated symptoms.

Bupleurum chinense exhibits antihypertensive properties, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and saponins. These compounds have been shown to reduce blood pressure and alleviate associated symptoms, potentially making it a useful treatment for conditions such as hypertension and cardiovascular disease.

parts_description: The most used parts of Bupleurum chinense for medicinal purposes are roots, rhizomes, leaves, and flowers. The roots and rhizomes of Bupleurum chinense are rich in flavonoids, saponins, and volatile oils, primarily exhibiting anti-inflammatory, hepatoprotective, and immunomodulatory properties. The leaves are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, primarily exhibiting antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The flowers contain flavonoids, alkaloids, and volatile oils, primarily exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and sedative properties. These medicinal properties are attributed to the bioactive compounds present in each part, which are responsible for their therapeutic effects.

parts_list.name: roots
parts_list.description: The roots of this plant are rich in saikosaponins, particularly saikosaponin d and a, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and hepatoprotective properties, providing relief from liver and gallbladder issues, as well as soothing digestive problems and alleviating fever.

parts_list.name: rhizomes
parts_list.description: The rhizomes of this plant are a primary source of saikosaponins, including saikosaponin d, a, and c, which possess hepatoprotective, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities, helping to alleviate liver damage, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress.

parts_list.name: leaves
parts_list.description: The leaves of this plant contain a variety of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, providing relief from fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems, as well as protecting against oxidative stress and inflammation.

parts_list.name: flowers
parts_list.description: The flowers of this plant are rich in flavonoids, particularly bupleurumol and quercetin, which possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral activities, helping to alleviate respiratory issues, reduce fever, and protect against oxidative stress and inflammation, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial properties against certain pathogens.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Tincture made from this plant is used to treat fever, inflammation, and respiratory infections, and to support liver function.
preparations.preparation_overview: Bupleurum chinense tincture is a traditional herbal remedy used to treat chronic hepatitis, liver cirrhosis, feverish cold, migraine, infectious diarrhea, respiratory tract infection and arthritis. It is widely used due to its efficacy and safety profile. The common effect of this tincture is its ability to clear heat and detoxify the body. This preparation is made from the roots, rhizomes and leaves of the Bupleurum chinense plant, which are often harvested and dried before being used to make the tincture. The process of making Bupleurum chinense tincture is moderately difficult, requiring careful selection of plant material and precise extraction methods to ensure the desired potency and quality.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh or dried roots and rhizomes of Bupleurum chinense, as well as its leaves, and clean them thoroughly.
Dry the gathered plant material to a moisture level of around 10% for optimal tincture quality.
Use a solvent such as ethanol or glycerin to macerate the dried roots, rhizomes, and leaves in a suitable container.
Allow the maceration to steep for several weeks, shaking the container regularly to facilitate extraction of the plant's active compounds.
Strain the liquid and discard the plant material, then bottle the resulting Bupleurum chinense herbal tincture for storage and use.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Decoction made from this plant is used to treat liver disorders, fever, and inflammatory diseases such as hepatitis and rheumatoid arthritis.
preparations.preparation_overview: Bupleurum chinense decoction is a traditional Chinese herbal remedy used to treat various conditions, including hepatitis, liver cirrhosis, rheumatism, gallstones, nephritis, fever, epilepsy, migraines, asthma, influenza, lymphoma, and hypertension, as well as certain types of cancer. This preparation is commonly used in traditional Chinese medicine due to its widespread availability and established usage. Its effects often include a reduction in inflammation and an improvement in liver function. Bupleurum chinense decoction is typically made from the roots, rhizomes, and leaves of the Bupleurum chinense plant. Moderately difficult to prepare, the decoction requires a combination of boiling and steeping the plant material to release its active compounds.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh or dried roots, rhizomes, and leaves of Bupleurum chinense.
Clean the roots, rhizomes, and leaves thoroughly under running water.
Combine the cleaned roots, rhizomes, and leaves in a large pot.
Pour in enough water to cover the Bupleurum chinense parts, then bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat and simmer the decoction for an extended period of time, allowing the active compounds to infuse into the water.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Capsule made from this plant is used to treat fever, liver and gallbladder disorders, and inflammation.
preparations.preparation_overview: Bupleurum chinense capsule is a traditional Chinese medicine preparation used to treat fever, hepatitis, jaundice, spleen, diarrhea, pneumonia, gallstones, and asthma. This preparation is widely used due to its efficacy in resolving various health issues. One of the common effects of Bupleurum chinense capsule is its ability to clear heat and detoxify the body. It is made from the roots and rhizomes of the Bupleurum chinense plant, which are harvested, processed, and then encapsulated for convenience and bioavailability. The preparation process for Bupleurum chinense capsule is moderately complex, requiring proper processing and quality control to ensure the desired medicinal properties are retained.
preparation_recipe: Harvest and clean the roots and rhizomes of Bupleurum chinense, ensuring they are free from impurities.
Dry the harvested roots and rhizomes thoroughly in a well-ventilated area or using a dehydrator.
Grind the dried roots and rhizomes into a fine powder using a grinding machine or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground powder with a suitable binding agent to form a uniform mixture, which will later be filled into capsules.
Fill the mixture into empty capsules, ensuring each capsule contains an equal amount of the Bupleurum chinense powder.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Tea made from this plant is traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and liver and gallbladder disorders, as well as to lower cholesterol levels and blood pressure.
preparations.preparation_overview: Bupleurum chinense tea is a traditional herbal remedy used to treat various conditions, including fever, hepatitis, liver disease, nausea, diarrhea, cough, rheumatism, gastritis and migraine. It is one of the most widely used traditional Chinese medicines, often combined with other herbs in formulas like Saiko-Keishi-to. The preparation has a common side effect of skin rash, and in high doses, it can cause liver damage. It is typically made from the roots, rhizomes and leaves of the Bupleurum chinense plant. The roots and rhizomes are usually dried and then used to make an infusion by steeping them in hot water, while the leaves may be added fresh or dried. Preparing Bupleurum chinense tea is relatively straightforward, as it involves boiling the plant materials in water and then straining the liquid to create a medicinal tea.
preparation_recipe: Gather clean and dry roots, rhizomes, and leaves of Bupleurum chinense.
Rinse the roots, rhizomes, and leaves under cold running water to remove any impurities.
Combine the roots, rhizomes, and leaves in a large pot and add enough water to cover them completely.
Bring the water to a boil, then reduce the heat and let the mixture simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the solids, then serve the Bupleurum chinense herbal tea hot or cold.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Salve made from this plant is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions such as eczema, and to reduce inflammation and pain.
preparations.preparation_overview: Bupleurum chinense salve is used to treat various skin conditions, including eczema, burns, psoriasis, tinea, boils, acne, skin infections, fungal infections and warts, as well as herpes. This herbal preparation is relatively uncommon in modern medicine due to its complex composition and limited availability. Notably, Bupleurum chinense salve has been found to exhibit a unique effect in reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. The salve is typically made from the roots and rhizomes of the Bupleurum chinense plant, which are rich in bioactive compounds. Making Bupleurum chinense salve is moderately difficult due to the specific requirements for preparing and combining the plant materials.
preparation_recipe: Gather and clean the roots and rhizomes of Bupleurum chinense, then dry them in a cool, dark place.
Grind the dried roots and rhizomes into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered Bupleurum chinense with a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture gently, stirring occasionally, until the oil has infused with the properties of the Bupleurum chinense.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container and allow it to cool before whipping it into a salve consistency.

side_effects_overview: The possible side effects of improperly using Bupleurum chinense are inducing liver damage, exacerbating gastrointestinal problems such as diarrhea and vomiting, worsens kidney function in individuals with pre-existing conditions, triggers allergic reactions including anaphylaxis and skin rashes, elevates liver enzymes and bilirubin levels, increases the risk of bleeding and bruising due to anticoagulant properties, aggravates existing cardiovascular conditions, causes photosensitivity and sensitivity to light, precipitates a decline in platelet count, and alters the balance of gut microbiota leading to an imbalance in the body's pH levels.

precautions.precaution_name: Consult a healthcare professional before use
precautions.precaution_list_description: Interactions with medications or underlying medical conditions may occur.

precautions.precaution_name: Use in moderation due to hepatotoxic risk
precautions.precaution_list_description: Bupleurum chinense contains saikosaponins, which have been shown to cause hepatotoxicity in high doses.
Bupleurum chinense contains volatile oils that stimulate uterine contractions.
Bupleurum chinense may cause hepatotoxicity, necessitating liver function monitoring.

precautions.precaution_name: Interact with medications, especially blood thinners
precautions.precaution_list_description: Bupleurum chinense may interact with medications like warfarin, increasing the risk of bleeding.
Prolonged administration of Bupleurum chinense may lead to liver damage or dysfunction.

precautions.precaution_name: Avoid in individuals with liver disease
precautions.precaution_list_description: Bupleurum chinense is contraindicated in individuals with liver disease due to potential hepatotoxicity.
Concomitant use with other medications may alter efficacy or increase risk of adverse effects

precautions.precaution_name: Be aware of potential allergic reactions
precautions.precaution_list_description: Some individuals may exhibit allergic responses to Bupleurum chinense, manifesting as skin irritation, respiratory issues, or other systemic reactions.
Taking excessive amounts may lead to liver damage and other adverse effects.

intro_study: According to a study published by "Journal of ethnopharmacology", Bupleurum chinense has been reported to have significant therapeutic effects in various diseases, including fever, inflammatory disorders, and liver disease. The phytochemicals present in this plant, such as triterpene saponins and flavonoids, have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, anti-tumor, and hepatoprotective activities. These compounds have also been found to have antimicrobial, immunoregulation, neuroregulation, and antidiabetic properties, highlighting the potential benefits of using Bupleurum chinense as a medicinal resource.

According to a study published by "Zhong yao cai = Zhongyaocai = Journal of Chinese medicinal materials", the test-tube plants of Bupleurum chinense exhibit excellent botanical and pharmacognostic characters, resulting in a higher uniformity and lower variability. This leads to a consistent pharmaceutical character, with a higher content of effective components in the roots, which is beneficial for its medicinal quality. The study also shows that the test-tube plants of Bupleurum chinense have a higher seed production, larger aerial part yield, and higher economic value compared to ordinary seed plants.
properties_description: The therapeutic properties of Bupleurum chinense are attributed to its bioactive compounds, which include flavonoids, saponins, and volatile oils. Bupleurum chinense exhibits anti-inflammatory properties due to the presence of saponins, which have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. Its antioxidant properties are attributed to flavonoids, which scavenge free radicals and protect against oxidative stress. The plant also possesses antiviral and antimicrobial activities, which are mediated by volatile oils that inhibit the growth of pathogens. Additionally, Bupleurum chinense has been found to possess analgesic, antipyretic, cardioprotective, hepatoprotective, immunomodulatory, and antihypertensive properties, making it a valuable herb in traditional Chinese medicine for treating various health conditions.
constituents_description: The healing constituents of Bupleurum chinense are primarily responsible for its medicinal properties. Bupleurum chinense contains various bioactive compounds including saponins which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects through inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and reactive oxygen species. The flavonoids present in Bupleurum chinense possess significant antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities by scavenging free radicals and modulating the activity of enzymes involved in inflammation. Coumarins in Bupleurum chinense exhibit anticoagulant and hepatoprotective activities by inhibiting the synthesis of vitamin K-dependent clotting factors and protecting against liver damage caused by oxidative stress. Additionally, Bupleurum chinense contains terpenoids and glycosides which contribute to its anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory mediators and modulating the activity of immune cells.
preparation_description: The most common herbal preparation of Bupleurum chinense for medicinal purposes are tinctures, which are typically made with the roots and rhizomes of the plant and used to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Bupleurum chinense decoctions are often used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat liver and gallbladder disorders, as they are believed to promote the flow of Qi and relieve stagnation. Capsules containing the dried root of Bupleurum chinense are commonly used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to their expectorant properties. Bupleurum chinense tea, made by steeping the roots in hot water, is traditionally used to treat colds, flu, and other upper respiratory tract infections. A salve prepared from the roots of Bupleurum chinense is used topically to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and dermatitis.
precautions_overview: The precautions to take before using Bupleurum chinense medicinally are to consult a healthcare professional before use, especially for individuals with a history of liver disease. It is essential to use Bupleurum chinense in moderation due to its hepatotoxic risk, which can lead to liver damage. Furthermore, pregnant and breastfeeding women should avoid using Bupleurum chinense altogether, as it may pose risks to the fetus or infant. While using Bupleurum chinense, it is crucial to monitor liver function regularly to prevent liver damage. Additionally, be cautious when combining Bupleurum chinense with medications, especially blood thinners, and long-term use should be approached with caution, and Bupleurum chinense should be used under close medical supervision, as potential allergic reactions may occur and recommended dosages should be followed carefully.
